rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ARTEMIS V STAHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For Deposit Of The Funds From The 401k, And The Supplemental Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) No receipt of 401(k) distribution has been filed; and 2) No supplemental inventory and appraisal listing 401(k) distribution has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for counsel to address the issues: no POS of the NOH on file; the copy of the deed filed is not recorded, so either a recorded copy or the last recorded deed must be filed; as the attorney is not waiving her fees, she will be asked to state the source of the payment; and transfer of the propery cannot be made to the Conservators until their Letters are amended to include the power to have care and custory of the Conservatee's property. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WILBUR FLEETWOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Attorney requests $16,993 in extraordinary fees. a. Attorneys request a blended rate of $477.33/hr, including $795-$835/hr for attorney Harris. b. The request for extraordinary fees includes almost 15 hours to meet with realtors, which appears excessive and to be the responsibility of the personal representative. c. $26,151 is reported to have been spent so far on ordinary services at a blended rate of $467/hr, which include: i. 3 hrs @ $795/hr ($2,385) to "draft email to clients concerning extraordinary fees." ii. ~$7,000 related to a petition for preliminary distribution (filed on 3/24/20), which was ultimately taken off calendar because a final distribution was filed. iii. ~$7,000 for services related to the initial preparation of the instant final distribution petition. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 7th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NANCY EILEEN MARSDEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed orders have been received. 2) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 3) No proof of service (Judicial Council form DE-115) is on file. 4) Paragraph 1 of the prayer for relief requests the Court's determination of the validity of the trust, but the caption of the petition does not include this relief. CRC 7.102. See also CRC 7.50 re notice. 5) Did the trustor leave a will? If so, please provide a copy. 6) Is the subject Citibank account community property or separate property? Please provide facts supporting either characterization. 7) Please provide a copy of the subject Citibank account statement covering a period including 02/01/2020. 8) Please provide the names and addresses of all persons who would be intestate heirs of the trustor under Probate Code section 6402. 9) Why was the trust document notarized two months after the trustor signed?

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Relief Under Probate Code 4541, Including Revocation Of Power Of Attorney

To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOIS MAY GINTJEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be heard with PES-20-303984. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
